metadata.description: Curated marketplace of Claude Code plugins — developer frameworks, productivity, accessibility, SEO, DevOps, and more. v1.11.1 ships rad-planner 4.5 — re-adds the `--assessment` read-only diagnostic flag (lost in a rebase between v4.3 and v4.4). Runs the internal assessor over project evidence and emits a structured report (project facts, canonical-doc coverage + staleness + validator pass/fail, drift signals, inferred next-step) without writing anything. The right flag when asking "where does this project stand?" before choosing an entry point. v1.11.0 retires rad-writer — its project-narrative use case is covered by rad-planner 4.4's `/project-story` (for canonical-doc projects) and rad-explain 0.1.0's narrate-project + ground-readme (for any repo); the AI-pattern audit and voice-profile capabilities are retired with the plugin (Claude.ai skill version at `skills/rad-writer/` is preserved as a separate distribution). v1.10.0 shipped rad-explain 0.1.0 — a new plugin for honest project explanation. Five skills (narrate-project, elevator-pitch, draft-pitch, explain-document, ground-readme) + two pure-stdlib Python validators (check-grounding, check-overpromise). Works on any repo. Pairs with rad-planner's /project-story for canonical-doc projects. v1.9.0 shipped rad-planner 4.4 — two new project-story synthesis skills (`/project-story` and `/refresh-story`). v1.8.2 shipped rad-planner 4.3 — M0.5 briefing reshape + conversational-continuation triggers. The M0.5 hard gate (v4.2) now renders as a prose-first briefing — Where we left off + Last accomplishment + Next logical step + Or you could start with — matching rad-session's /startup doorman pattern. Same checkpoint, friendlier framing, layperson-readable. Rendering rules confine ADR numbers, schema column names, and bit numbers to a single Mechanical state line; the friendly framing stays in plain English. Trigger phrases expanded to catch free-form planning-continuation utterances at session open (continue the plan, continue planning, let's keep planning, pick up the plan, where did we leave off in the plan, resume the plan) — closes the gap where free-form planning continuation became ad-hoc agent work instead of routing through /plan --improve. v1.8.1 shipped rad-planner 4.2 + rad-session 5.3 — the user-confirmation-surface pair. rad-planner 4.2 adds an **M0.5 scope-confirmation hard gate** between mechanical M0 discovery and any substantive work: surfaces a terse discovery summary + the agent's inferred scope + three options (confirm / different scope with depth menu / redirect). Fires on ALL entry points and ALL invocation modes including `--auto` (autonomy applies to the work, not to the scope decision). Closes a defect where `/plan --improve` correctly parked canonical doc emission but still produced 9-sub-section consolidation work without asking what scope the user wanted. rad-session 5.3 adds a **floor of one line on /startup** — silent completion violates the doorman model; even the cleanest routine open emits a confirmation line with status freshness, plan progress, and a pointer to docs/status.md. Anomalies stack above the floor; the floor always appears. Both patches encode the same principle: confirm the skill ran, surface what it sees, hand the next move back to the user. v1.8.0 shipped rad-planner 4.1 + rad-session 5.2 (permission-mode-safety pair). v1.7.1 shipped rad-session 5.1 (/init → /startup Phase 0.5 fold). v1.7.0 shipped rad-session 5.0 (canonical doc structure). v1.6.0 shipped rad-planner 4.0 (plan-first workflow).

plugins.name: rad-code-review
plugins.description: 3-role adversarial code review with diff-aware scoping and AI slop detection — blame-aware diff filtering, framework-specific IDOR heuristics, 14 AI slop patterns, performance profiling, WCAG 2.2 dynamic ARIA state detection, interactive findings management with accept/filter/fix workflow, and severity-ranked findings with optional fix application.

plugins.name: rad-chrome-extension
plugins.description: Comprehensive MV3 Chrome extension development standards — WXT framework architecture, MV3 security, permission minimization, typed messaging, storage patterns, service worker lifecycle, React UI, testing, and Chrome Web Store troubleshooting.

plugins.name: rad-a11y
plugins.description: Digital accessibility toolkit for WCAG 2.2 AA compliance — semantic HTML, ARIA authoring practices, keyboard navigation, focus management, color contrast, motion/animation, form accessibility, SVG accessibility, and automated testing with axe-core.

plugins.name: rad-website-design
plugins.description: Vision implementer for 2026 web design — translates a vision into code that meets modern standards, and proposes concrete fixes for existing sites. Nine skills across knowledge layer (must-do baseline, should-do recommendations, severity-tiered anti-patterns with damage cited from real research, aesthetic direction with Calm UX default, agentic UX patterns Intent Preview/Autonomy Dial/Action Audit, AEO/llms.txt) and action layer (implement = vision-to-code, improve = identify+propose fixes for source or live URLs, review = pure static report). One autonomous reviewer agent, three pure-stdlib Python validators (viewport-meta, pure-bw, tap-targets), six slash commands. Honest scope: does not brainstorm or plan (use rad-brainstormer / rad-planner upstream), does not run Lighthouse, does not take screenshots, does not render JS SPAs. Pair with chrome-devtools-mcp for runtime/visual layer.

plugins.name: rad-supabase
plugins.description: End-to-end Supabase development and operations — projects, database, migrations, edge functions, auth, storage, realtime, branching, security, and troubleshooting. Covers both Supabase CLI and Supabase MCP tool usage.

plugins.name: rad-coolify-orchestrator
plugins.description: End-to-end Coolify self-hosted PaaS management — deployments, databases, security, CI/CD, troubleshooting, observability, and multi-server infrastructure. Coolify v4 self-hosted.

plugins.name: rad-1password
plugins.description: End-to-end 1Password CLI (op) coverage — secret references (op://...), `op inject`/`op run`/`op read` for keeping secrets out of code and shell history, item CRUD with assignment statements and JSON templates, vault and user provisioning with granular permissions, SSH key generation and use, third-party CLI auth via `op plugin run`, and service-account / Connect-server patterns for CI/CD and headless servers. 1 router skill, 6 topical sub-skills, 4 slash commands. Cross-checked against op v2.34.0 --help.

plugins.name: rad-brainstormer
plugins.description: Collaborative ideation using proven methodologies (SCAMPER, Six Hats, Reverse Brainstorming, 5 Whys, HMW), domain research, structured evaluation, and design sprints.

plugins.name: rad-seo-optimizer
plugins.description: Comprehensive SEO auditing, AEO/GEO optimization, competitor intelligence, keyword discovery, link building, content strategy, schema markup, and E-E-A-T optimization.

plugins.name: rad-context-prompter
plugins.description: Production-grade prompt engineering toolkit — systematic tool-specific routing across 30+ AI platforms, anti-pattern diagnostics, technique selection matrix, prompt decompilation, autonomous debugging, context engineering for agentic systems, and reusable templates.

plugins.name: rad-para-second-brain
plugins.description: Active PARA system management — weekly review automation, folder structure auditing, Progressive Summarization, Intermediate Packet assembly, Hemingway Bridge session handoffs, and 12 Favorite Problems workshop.

plugins.name: rad-session
plugins.description: **5.3** — Make agent sessions remember each other across machines AND across agents (Claude + Codex). Three skills (`/startup`, `/wrapup`, `/add-resource`) built around the canonical doc structure: operating manual conditional on agent scope (CLAUDE.md and/or AGENTS.md), `docs/status.md` (evidence-based reality), `docs/planning/current.md` (intent; rad-planner-owned), `docs/planning/archive/` (shipped-milestone journal), `docs/decisions/`. **Floor of one line on /startup (v5.3):** the skill MUST produce at least one user-visible line even in the cleanest routine case — silent completion violates the doorman model. Routine confirmation line includes status freshness + plan progress + pointer to docs/status.md. Anomalies stack above; the floor always appears. **Permission-mode-safe (v5.2):** operations split by asymmetry of downside. `/startup` Phase 0.5 Case C guard rail is data-loss-protected — prompts regardless of Auto / Bypass / `--non-interactive`. `/wrapup` Phase 3 `--auto` mode writes draft-banner ADRs so captures aren't lost; review surfaces in next `/startup`. **`/startup` Phase 0.5 auto-bootstraps on first run** (v5.1) — stack detection, agent_scope question, Operational sections per sectioned-writer rule, configs per scope, plugin-bloat audit. In steady state, `/startup` is read-only and targets <5s — doorman model. `/wrapup` writes `docs/status.md` from EVIDENCE (git diff, test output, plan-task state). Mode-aware. Anomaly-gated final output. Cross-machine continuity built-in. Pairs with rad-planner 4.2; works standalone.

plugins.name: rad-gws-core
plugins.description: Google Workspace core integration — 14 essential skills covering Gmail (send, read, reply, triage), Docs (write), Sheets (read, append), Slides, Drive, and Calendar. Requires the gws CLI binary.

plugins.name: rad-planner
plugins.description: **4.5** — Plan-first project planning, **conversational by design**. **v4.5 exposes a read-only `--assessment` flag** that runs the internal assessor over whatever evidence exists (git, docs, source, validator results) and emits a structured report: project facts, canonical-doc coverage + staleness + validator pass/fail, detected drift signals, and an inferred next-step suggestion (full / improve / drift / pivot / `no planner action needed`) with one-sentence rationale. Distinct from `--drift` (which requires an existing plan): `--assessment` works on any project state, produces no writes, skips M0.5 and M1–M5. Useful before choosing an entry point. **v4.4 adds two project-story synthesis skills** (`/project-story` and `/refresh-story`) that derive plain-language narrative views from the canonical doc set: state-of-project summary for non-developer stakeholders, new collaborators, future-self after time away, or funders. The narrative format is validated against a real use case (one-line product test, who it's for in build order, what it's explicitly NOT, where we are, what's pending, bottom line). `/refresh-story` updates an existing story file in place via inline drift detection — regenerates only sections whose canonical source has changed; preserves user edits in fresh sections. Plain English, no marketing language, every claim traceable to source. **v4.3** reshapes the M0.5 surface from a terse identifier-dense recap into a prose-first briefing matching rad-session's /startup doorman model — Where we left off + Last accomplishment + Next logical step + Or you could start with (2–3 concrete alternatives including a skip / execute / redirect option) + a single Mechanical state line for identifiers. v4.3 also expands trigger phrases to catch free-form planning-continuation utterances at session open (continue the plan, continue planning, pick up the plan, resume the plan). The **M0.5 scope-confirmation hard gate** (v4.2) fires after M0 discovery and BEFORE any substantive work on ALL entry points and ALL modes including `--auto`. M1–M5 require explicit user dialogue (v4.1) regardless of harness-level autonomy hints. New `--auto` flag (v4.1) is the only way to suppress M1–M5 dialogue; it still respects M0.5; produces DRAFT-labeled strawmen and does NOT write ADRs. Four entry points (full plan / improvement / drift assessment / pivot) plus a universal four-direction menu as ambiguity fallback. Five-phase conversation (Constitution & Frame → Goal-Backward Scope → Sequence with Size Discipline → Quality Gates → Doc-Set Recommendation) produces the plan; M6 executes the approved doc set per the canonical structure. Four mechanical validators back the workflow: plan-lint, status-validator, doc-redundancy, doc-contradiction. Pairs with rad-session 5.3 for the full lifecycle; works standalone.

plugins.name: rad-explain
plugins.description: **0.1.0** — Honest project explanation for any repo. Five skills generate audience-targeted external communications from a project's internal artifacts (code, docs, planning files): `narrate-project` (plain-English description, audience-adaptive — non-dev / investor / future-self / new-collaborator); `elevator-pitch` (~150 words / ~30 seconds spoken); `draft-pitch` (one-pager / executive summary / deck-equivalent for funding, grants, partnerships); `explain-document` (interpret a specific repo file — "what does this commit me to?", "what does this assume?", "where could this go wrong?"); `ground-readme` (generate or audit a README / marketplace listing so it doesn't overpromise — two modes: audit existing or generate new). Two pure-stdlib Python validators run on every skill's output: `check-grounding.py` (every substantive claim must trace to repo source; proper-noun-shaped tokens checked strictly, other-substantive tokens loosely) and `check-overpromise.py` (flag superlatives, vague-quantity claims without enumeration, marketing fluff, production-readiness assertions without evidence, filler intensifiers; backing-aware downgrade). Works on any repo — no rad-planner dependency. Reads canonical `docs/` if present, falls back to README + project manifest + source structure when absent. Pairs with `/rad-planner:project-story` for canonical-doc-set narratives. Eats own dog food on the marketplace's no-sensational-copy guardrail — the rad-explain README itself passes its own `check-overpromise.py`.
